item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The flaw with your argument is that SCP-4213 cannot be disobeyed once it itself is perceived, not its command. That means as soon as you're aware SCP-4213 exists, you can't disobey it, even if you did not hear its command.

Additionally, getting large amounts of ice cream isn't an easy feat, and considering it seems to constantly pursue ice cream, even a small shortage of it would cause SCP-4213 to breach containment, potentially disrupting many staff duties in its attempt to facilitate more ice cream.

Considering that this is a relatively new anomaly the Foundation discovered, they haven't nailed down the proper procedures, and there isn't much rush since it doesn't require that many resources to contain.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Regular skips are allowed to be funny.

then the idea of having a mind controlling scp isn't new nor novel, even with the twist that he only wants ice cream

Very true. It's all in the execution, which seems to have paid off well by the authors if the vote count is any indication.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't understand how this is not a -j scp, clearly underwhelming when compared to the standart I have for other scps, the fact that he is uncontained is ridiculous, even if newly discovered, the foundation would be able to contain a thousand of him, you just need to give him ice cream and he'll follow you, place an ice cream machine in his cell, make sound proof walls and make everyone who come in have ear protections, make him unconcious…
then the idea of having a mind controlling scp isn't new nor novel, even with the twist that he only wants ice cream, which would mostly belong in a -j

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It goes where the ice-cream is and makes people hand over the ice-cream. That is an easy to grasp concept, but How does it know where to go to get the ice-cream? How does the MTF track the SCP without making audio or visual contact. Do they have a tracker on it? I'm taking this SCP far to seriously.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: i like this article, i really do, but it's actually paced like a -J. it has a punchline and everything. i'm still gonna upvote Free Ice Cream Man, obviously, just… i dunno. doesn't feel like a mainlister. and that's really my only gripe.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"Free Ice Cream Day"

You had me upvoting as soon as I read this, and I stand by that decision.
Having said that, this article feels like it's full of untapped potential. To begin with, we don't have enough material to properly characterise that madlad nor have you delved into the depths of the comedic well you have here. You're dipping your toes in on the surface level, and it's a lovely experience. But it would be even better if we could swim a little deeper. As long as we don't drown, this would be an exceptionally funny article beyond just something worth upvoting.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nope. He's not "humanoid," he's human.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A great scp!
Although shouldn't the foundation refer to him as a "humanoid entity" and not as a "man of tibetan decent" ?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: my mans just wants some ice cream, mad respect

item.title: ice cream
item.content:encoded:
man just wants some ice cream
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So the guy is just Purple Man but apathetic and constantly wants ice cream. Not sure if this would have passed five years ago.

item.title: Epic
item.content:encoded: I love this. That cunt could do anything, but instead, he decides to use… ice cream? I whole-heartedly laughed at this quirky article. +1 from me!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Pretty bad, take my very first down. I'm sorry because this collaboration represents surely many hours of work, but I think it would be better to delete it (at least until a better rewriting).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The Foundation should just put an ice cream machine into SCP-4213's containment cell. Sort of feel bad for him, unsure why.

item.title: One hundred!
item.content:encoded: When Cimmerian and I were working on this SCP, we thought it'd end up between 60–80 upvotes. Well, here we are at 100! Thank you everyone who read and liked the skip! :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is irredeemably absurd. That said, upvoted, and congrats on your +100 :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I didn't give you permission to write a skip about me!1

Anyway I like this. Fucker just wants ice cream! +1

Footnotes
1. Clearly a joke and I am saddened by the world that I feel the need to add this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There is no inside joke, in case you're still curious.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It really doesn't land for me. The joke feels pretty insipid (Although I may be missing an inner joke. In which case even worse because then the article cannot stand on its own.) and it fails at being interesting. It's surprising that so many people like it, but again, I might me missing an inner joke.

I'll keep my vote as negative, but since the article will be updated, I'll check it every now and again to see if it "wins me."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+ Plot Twist
- DUN DUN DUNNNN

Whoever 4213 interacts with becomes lactose-intolerant.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Upvote here. I love stuff thats difficult to maintain but generally harmless…for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: ..It’s nice, but it doesn’t really go anywhere and the joke didn’t really land for me. I can see why others would like it, though. Nonvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I imagine at some point they'll try that (once they have a better understanding of the anomaly). They'll fail of course, since the anomaly will somehow still seek out ice cream when unconscious. But they don't know that yet.

Just a note: this is short because it's a recently discovered anomaly. They're still getting their bearings since it was discovered like a month ago.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't understand how he cannot be contained. If people retain their free will until he tells them to do something, couldn't they just tranquilize him?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
disregard free will, acquire ice cream

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"Let be be finale of seem/
The only emperor is the emperor of ice cream."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: How is this so seemingly harmless yet so unnerving? Take your +1 and leave my ice cream alone.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Novoted. Personally, I don't find this concept very funny, but I like the fact that this man can possibly make others do pretty much anything, yet he doesn't care about anything other than ice cream.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Conclusive evidence that keter class skips can be relatively benign, and absolutely hilarious!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Am i the only one that found this funny, yes, but also quite unsettling? I mean this guy could do anything were just lucky hes a little… weird.

Also i love that its called an "acquisition event".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The fact that it's not even classified as a -J SCP makes this all the more funnier

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This was fun to work on! I'm glad I got the opportunity to collaborate with you!

Thank yous to Westrin and RockTeethMothEyes for getting a quick look at this draft and giving it some critique in its earlier form!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is stupid and really, really funny. One man's ruthless and unstoppable quest for…ice cream.

The MTF is the best. I'd love to see someone find another SCP idea that would involve them.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This feels….very familiar….anyone from #Site19 might see where I'm coming from.

Regardless +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't really know about this one. The man's obsession with ice cream is absurd enough as it is, but there's not enough to really pick apart.

It's really just a guy that can telepathically control people into feeding it more ice cream and it isn't interesting enough to leave an impact or impression on me.

Eta: On second thought, I upvoted articles like Salsa Hammer, so it would be hypocritical of me to give that one a pass.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Fun idea I had with Yossi in Discord and which became this. It's small, it's simple, and of course it could take over the world. You're welcome.

Image is from here: https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Man_with_Ice_Cream_-_The_Mall_-_Shimla_-_Himachal_Pradesh_-_India_(26460945951).jpg
